mnemonics: R.U.C.A.B.L.E. - Recoverable schedules Undo Cascading Aborts Before Lost Effects
keyPoints: A schedule is recoverable if a transaction commits only after all transactions whose changes it read have committed.
A schedule avoids cascading aborts (is ACA) if a transaction only reads data written by committed transactions.
A schedule is strict if a transaction neither reads nor overwrites data written by an uncommitted transaction.
commonMistakes: Assuming that a conflict-serializable schedule is automatically recoverable (it is not).
Confusing Cascading Aborts (a bad thing) with Cascading Rollback (the consequence of it).
Thinking that 'Strict' is only about writes; it also restricts reading uncommitted data.
